item.title: US Gas Market Sees Volatility With Shifting Weather and Oil Price Moves
item.description: US natural gas futures fluctuated between slight gains and losses as traders factored another bearish weather forecast against rising oil prices, which have pulled up the US gas contract since the war in Iran began.

item.title: Roblox’s Hit Game ‘Steal A Brainrot’ Battles Its Many Imitators
item.description: In the popular video game Steal A Brainrot, the object is petty theft. But now that the internet is awash with knockoffs of the runaway Roblox Corp. hit, its makers are defending their turf.

item.title: Trian and General Catalyst Raise Janus Offer to $52 a Share
item.description: Trian Fund Management and General Catalyst raised their offer for Janus Henderson Group Plc to $52 a share in cash as they fend off a rival bid from Victory Capital Holdings.

item.title: Ireland Announces Energy Support Package to Ease Iran War Impact
item.description: Ireland’s government approved a €250 million ($290 million) package to alleviate the impact of soaring energy costs caused by the Iran war, including a cut to the fuel excise duty.

item.title: UK-Swiss Trade Agreement Hinges on Market Access, Data and IP
item.description: A new free-trade agreement between the UK and Switzerland must overcome issues such as data regulation, intellectual property and access to service-sector companies, the Swiss government said while stressing that talks are “well advanced.”

item.title: Top US Natural Gas Drillers Seek to Remove Middlemen From Sales
item.description: Expand Energy Corp. and EQT Corp. are trying to displace traders that act as middlemen so the biggest US natural gas drillers can reap bigger profits from their output.

item.title: Uber Expands NYC Office Following CEO Move, In-Office Mandate
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. is expanding its New York offices, now the biggest outside its San Francisco headquarters, a reflection of the city’s growing importance to the company’s operations since Chief Executive Officer Dara Khosrowshahi moved there in recent years.

item.title: Pimco Takes Contrarian View as Market Bets on Global Rate Hikes
item.description: Pacific Investment Management Co. is touting “opportunities to invest against the prevailing narrative” as war in the Middle East and a hawkish shift in expectations for global central banks rattles markets.

item.title: Ares, Apollo Fall as Firms Curb Private Credit Fund Redemptions
item.description: Shares of alternative asset managers declined after Ares Management Corp. and Apollo Global Management Inc. became the latest firms to curb withdrawals from some of their funds.

item.title: Iran Charges Some Ships Hormuz Transit Fees for Safe Passage
item.description: Iran has started charging transit fees on some commercial vessels passing through the Strait of Hormuz, another sign of Tehran’s control over the world’s most important maritime energy channel.

item.title: Odey Says Receptionists May Have Thought He Was Just a ‘Creepy Old Man’
item.description: Ex-hedge fund boss Crispin Odey, facing allegations that he harassed two female receptionists, told a London court that while he thought his advances may have been acceptable when he was younger by the time he was in his 60s the women likely thought he was a “creepy old man.”

item.title: Ares Limits Private Credit Fund Withdrawals as Redemptions Surge
item.description: Ares is limiting redemptions at its $10.7 billion private credit fund as alternative asset managers face a surge in such requests.

item.title: Crusoe Buys Batteries That Last for Days to Power New Data Centers
item.description: The deal with Form Energy reflects growing interest in a niche technology that can help provide power when prices are high or when the grid is under stress.

item.title: Russia Pauses Budget Rule Change as Oil Rally Eases Fiscal Woes
item.description: Russia is delaying a decision aimed at reducing its dependence on energy income as oil prices surge on the back of the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Lufthansa Bookings Surge With Carrier Shifting Capacity to Asia
item.description: Deutsche Lufthansa AG is seeing a significant increase in bookings for March departures as the war in the Middle East disrupts operations for Gulf-based carriers, Chief Financial Officer Till Streichert said.

item.title: Mexico Prices Jump More Than Expected Before Rate Decision
item.description: Mexico’s annual inflation accelerated much more than expected in early March, raising the stakes for the central bank’s interest rate meeting Thursday with the war in the Middle East now in its fourth week.

item.title: Trump’s Oil Market Messaging Runs Into Geopolitical Reality
item.description: The war has shown how much control Iran can exert over the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: BlackRock’s Rieder Seeks Cash for Hedge Fund After Fed Race
item.description: BlackRock Inc.’s Rick Rieder, the firm’s top bond-market executive who was passed over by President Donald Trump to be chair of the Federal Reserve, is pursuing investor cash for his first hedge fund in years.

item.title: French Nuclear Helps Shield Europe Power Prices Amid Iran Shock
item.description: France is on track for its highest level of nuclear generation for March since 2019, helping to support power markets across Europe amid the energy shock caused by the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Updates From CERAWeek Energy Conference
item.description: Signage at the CERAWeek by S&P Global conference in Houston on March 23. Photographer: F. Carter Smith/Bloomberg

item.title: Kleiner Perkins Raises $3.5 Billion to Invest in the AI Boom
item.description: Kleiner Perkins, the half-century-old venture capital institution, is raising $3.5 billion to make bets on artificial intelligence startups reshaping industries including software, health care, transportation and autonomy.

item.title: Europe Shows Early Signs of the Iran War’s Economic Costs
item.description: Services industries in Germany, the UK and France suffered setbacks as fighting extends to fourth week

item.title: Drones, Spacecraft and Energy: VC Overmatch Raises $250 Million for Defense Bets
item.description: With more global conflicts brewing and artificial intelligence reshaping modern warfare, defense tech investing is ascendant. That’s presented an opportunity for Overmatch Ventures, which is raising a $250 million fund to bet on startups working on products like drones, spacecraft and portable renewable energy.

item.title: CME, Bank of Montreal Team Up to Launch Tokenized Cash Service
item.description: Bank of Montreal plans to offer tokenized cash services and deposits to institutional clients, allowing them to move money around more easily at any time.

item.title: Brazil Central Bank Warns Iran War Is Stoking Inflation Risks
item.description: Brazil’s central bank warned that inflation risks intensified after war in the Middle East broke out and said future steps in its monetary policy will be determined as new information is incorporated.

item.title: Neom Scraps Steel Contract With Malaysia Firm on Mideast Crisis
item.description: Malaysia’s Eversendai Corporation Bhd. said its structural steel contract for Trojena Ski Village, an ambitious desert ski resort that’s part of Saudi Arabia’s planned megacity of Neom, has been terminated amid escalating tensions in the region.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: Jefferies, Netgear, Applied Optoelectronics
item.description: US equity-index futures slip as traders grapple with a wide range of possible outcomes for the war in the Middle East. Contracts on the S&P 500 Index are down 0.2% at 7:45 a.m. in New York.

item.title: China’s $1.6 Trillion Fund Rekindles Ties With US Money Managers
item.description: China’s $1.57 trillion sovereign wealth fund - long one of the biggest backers of private equity firms in the world - is considering new allocations to US money managers just months after reducing its exposure to the world’s biggest economy.

item.title: VIX Signals Traders Want Proof It’s Safe to Buy the S&P 500 Dip
item.description: As stocks staged a powerful rally on Monday after President Donald Trump eased off his threats on Iran, the message among some money managers was clear: don’t get comfortable just yet.

item.title: US Stocks Futures Hold Steady as Oil Rises With Conflict Unease
item.description: Stock futures held steady as oil climbed with fighting between the US-Israel alliance and Iran raging unabated.
